sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Wolverhampton, sitting at 13th in the league with 41 points, host Leicester City, who are struggling in 19th place with just 21 points, in Round 34 at the Molineux Stadium in Wolverhampton. This clash presents a significant opportunity for Wolves to capitalize on their home advantage against a Leicester side desperate for points to climb out of the relegation zone. The referee for this match, Samuel Barrott, averages 3.7 yellow cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form and Streaks
sections.text: Wolverhampton are in fine form, riding a streak of 5 consecutive wins and remaining unbeaten in their last 6 matches. They've also been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 games, indicating a strong start to matches. On the other hand, Leicester City are in a dire run, winless in their last 11 games and without a clean sheet in a staggering 29 consecutive matches. Additionally, Leicester have been the first to concede in their last 9 games, pointing to early defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Wolverhampton have the edge with 3 wins compared to Leicester's 2, with no draws recorded. Their most recent encounter on December 22, 2024, saw Wolves dominate with a 3-0 victory away at Leicester, where goalkeeper José Sá was named Player of the Match with an impressive 8.0 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head trends also show that 4 of the last 5 games have had more than 2.5 goals, suggesting a high likelihood of goals in this fixture, while 6 of the last 8 matches have had fewer than 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Wolverhampton
sections.text: Wolverhampton’s attack is spearheaded by Matheus Cunha, who boasts an average Sofascore rating of 7.65, ranking 3rd in the league, and has scored 15 goals with 6 assists this season. Cunha is also frequently fouled, averaging 2.3 fouls drawn per game, which ranks 2nd league-wide, potentially earning free kicks in dangerous areas. Jørgen Strand Larsen, with 13 goals, is another threat, especially in form with 5 goals in his last 5 matches. Midfielder Rayan Aït Nouri also contributes with 4 goals and 7 assists, adding creativity from the middle of the park.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Leicester City
sections.text: Leicester City will rely heavily on veteran forward Jamie Vardy, who has scored 8 goals this season despite underperforming his expected goals of 10.75. Jordan Ayew, with 5 goals, provides another attacking option, while midfielder Wilfred Ndidi adds dynamism with 4 assists and an average rating of 6.97. However, Leicester are missing key players like Abdul Fatawu Issahaku, whose 2.6 successful dribbles per game rank 3rd in the league, which could hamper their attacking flair.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Styles
sections.text: Wolverhampton have scored 51 goals this season, ranking 12th in the league, but have underperformed their expected goals by nearly 10, placing them 19th in over/underperformance. They excel defensively in ground duels, winning 51.54% (6th in the league), and lead the league with 21.17 tackles per game. However, their 62 goals conceded (17th worst) and low possession average of 47.23% (15th) indicate vulnerabilities. Leicester’s stats are less encouraging, with a porous defense conceding in every game for 29 matches, and their inability to control games evident in their first-to-concede streak.

sections.subtitle: Scoring Patterns and Timing
sections.text: Wolverhampton tend to score most frequently between the 31st and 45th minutes and the 61st to 75th minutes, with each period accounting for 19.35% of their goals. Late goals are also common, with 17.74% scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. Leicester City, meanwhile, peak between the 16th and 30th minutes, scoring 22.37% of their goals, and also find the net often late in games, with 21.05% between the 76th and 90th minutes. Both teams’ late-game tendencies could lead to a dramatic finish.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Outlook
sections.text: Wolverhampton rank 2nd in the league for fouls committed with 457, and their 72 yellow cards place them 8th for least cautions, averaging 2.06 per game. With referee Samuel Barrott’s tendency to issue 3.7 yellow cards per game, expect some bookings. Wolves struggle with corners, averaging just 3.54 per game (20th in the league), while head-to-head stats suggest fewer than 10.5 corners in this matchup. Leicester’s ongoing defensive woes may also lead to fouls under pressure, contributing to card counts.
